mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly employment gains (PAYEMS) indicate robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in >60% of instances with PAYEMS growth above 100k, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) reflect strong demand for residential construction, signaling economic expansion that supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX records negative 20d returns in >60% of periods with HOUST growth above 5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing industrial production (INDPRO) indicates stronger manufacturing activity that boosts corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 20d returns in >60% of INDPRO growth periods, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 4.5% signals monetary tightening that raises discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX records positive 20d returns in >60% of FEDFUNDS >4.5% periods, or Rank IC > 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicates strong economic momentum that drives corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 60d returns in >60% of combined GDPC1 growth and UNRATE decline periods, or interaction effect size < 0.15 in rolling 3-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 0.5% signals an ongoing monetary tightening cycle that raises borrowing costs and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX records positive 20d returns in >60% of FEDFUNDS tightening periods, or Rank IC > 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 105 signals weakening manufacturing activity that reduces corporate earnings and generates negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 5d returns in >60% of INDPRO decline periods, or Rank IC > 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real sales (RSAFS) indicate stronger consumer demand that boosts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX records negative 20d returns in >60% of RSAFS growth periods, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
